UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores core information about a user, including their role, creation, and last activity timestamps. This entity links to other user-specific data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

role.description: The assigned role of the user within the application (e.g., 'standard', 'admin').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

lastSeen.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user was active in the application.

modeDefault.description: The identifier of the default dashboard mode set for this user (e.g., 'MinimalMode').

UserPreference.title: UserPreference
UserPreference.description: Stores user-specific application preferences and visual settings, including details for schema versioning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserPreference entity.

schemaVersion.description: The version of the user preference schema, used for migration purposes.

lastMigratedAt.description: Timestamp of the last successful migration of this preference schema.

visualProfileMotion.description: User's preference for UI animations and motion (e.g., 'reduced', 'standard').

visualProfileContrast.description: User's preference for color contrast in the UI (e.g., 'standard', 'high').

visualProfileFontScaling.description: User's preference for font scaling, represented as a multiplier or percentage.

UserLayout.title: UserLayout
UserLayout.description: Defines a user's customized dashboard layout for a specific mode, including panel configurations and grid settings, supporting schema versioning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLayout entity.

modeId.description: Identifier of the mode this layout applies to (e.g., 'MinimalMode', 'ProDeskMode').

schemaVersion.description: The version of the layout schema, used for migration purposes.

layoutId.description: A unique identifier for this specific layout instance within the user's modes.

lastMigratedAt.description: Timestamp of the last successful migration of this layout schema.

panelConfig.description: An array of configurations for each panel displayed within this layout.

gridConfig.description: Configuration string or JSON representing the grid layout properties (e.g., column count, row height, cell sizes).

ExperimentAssignment.title: ExperimentAssignment
ExperimentAssignment.description: Records a user's assignment to an A/B test experiment or feature flag variant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExperimentAssignment entity.

experimentId.description: Identifier of the experiment or feature flag (e.g., 'onboarding_variant').

variantId.description: The specific variant assigned to the user within the experiment (e.g., 'workflow_copy', 'brain_ui_copy').

assignedAt.description: Timestamp when the user was assigned to this variant.

Feedback.title: Feedback
Feedback.description: Stores user-submitted feedback, issues, or 'confusion points' for application improvement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Feedback entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the feedback or issue.

eventContext.description: Optional contextual information about the user's activity or UI state when the feedback was submitted (e.g., current URL, mode ID).

submittedAt.description: Timestamp when the feedback was submitted.

UserEvent.title: UserEvent
UserEvent.description: Logs significant user actions and events within the application, such as onboarding step completion or mode usage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserEvent entity.

eventType.description: The type of event that occurred (e.g., 'onboarding_step_completed', 'mode_selected', 'panel_added').

eventDetails.description: Optional JSON string containing specific details relevant to the event (e.g., {'step': 3}, {'mode': 'ProDeskMode'}).

definition.description: Stores core information about a user, including their role, creation, and last activity timestamps. The '{userId}' parameter matches 'request.auth.uid' for direct ownership and access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores user-specific application preferences and visual settings. Access is restricted to the user whose ID matches '{userId}'. Denormalized 'userId' within the path ensures authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: preferenceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific set of user preferences (e.g., 'main').

definition.description: Defines a user's customized dashboard layout for a specific mode. Access is restricted to the user whose ID matches '{userId}'. Denormalized 'userId' within the path ensures authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: layoutId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user layout within a mode.

definition.description: Records a user's assignment to an A/B test experiment or feature flag variant. Access is restricted to the user whose ID matches '{userId}'. Denormalized 'userId' within the path ensures authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific experiment assignment.

definition.description: Logs significant user actions and events within the application. Access is restricted to the user whose ID matches '{userId}'. Denormalized 'userId' within the path ensures authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user event.

definition.description: Stores user-submitted feedback. Documents include the 'userId' field, which is denormalized within the document for authorization independence for individual feedback items. Allows creation by any authenticated user for their own 'userId', and read/update/delete by the owning user. Listing typically restricted to administrators.

params.name: feedbackId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific feedback submission. May incorporate user ID and timestamp for uniqueness (e.g., '{userId}_{timestamp}').

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, prioritizing authorization independence and supporting efficient queries (QAPs). It leverages a hierarchical, path-based ownership model for all user-specific data, aligning with the "Private Data" and "Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data" access modeling strategies. 

**Authorization Independence & Denormalization:**
All user-owned data entities ã `UserProfile`, `UserPreference`, `UserLayout`, `ExperimentAssignment`, and `UserEvent` ã are nested directly under a `users/{userId}` path (e.g., `users/{userId}/preferences/{preferenceId}`). This design makes the `userId` an inherent part of the document's path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules to ascertain ownership. Authorization rules for these collections can simply check `request.auth.uid == userId`, making them atomic, easy to write, and highly debuggable. This structure directly supports atomic creation of parent/child documents by not requiring `get()` calls for authorization, fulfilling the CRITICAL Authorization Independence mandate.

For the `Feedback` entity, which is designed as a top-level collection (`feedback/{feedbackId}`), authorization independence for individual document ownership is achieved by including the `userId` field directly within each `Feedback` document. This denormalized `userId` allows security rules to verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` for read/update/delete operations on an individual feedback item without needing to access a parent document.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) & Structural Segregation:**

1.  **User-Specific Collections (`users/{userId}/...`):** For collections like `preferences`, `layouts`, `experimentAssignments`, and `userEvents`, the path-based ownership (`users/{userId}/...`) naturally enforces query scoping. Any `list` operation on these subcollections will inherently be limited to documents belonging to the `userId` specified in the path, which must match `request.auth.uid`. This allows for secure and performant `list` operations without relying on security rules to filter results, thereby adhering to QAPs.

2.  **Global/Admin-Reviewable Collection (`feedback/{feedbackId}`):** The `feedback` collection is designed to be a top-level collection. Regular users are permitted to *create* feedback (ensuring `request.resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`) and can read/update/delete *their own* specific feedback document if they know its ID. However, regular users are explicitly **not allowed to `list`** the entire `/feedback` collection. This prevents broad, unconstrained queries and upholds QAPs. For administrative access, the design accommodates `list` and `read` operations for users identified as administrators. 

**DBAC and Admin Role Handling (Mandate Compromise):**
Due to the constraints of the provided schema and the JSON output format (which requires `$ref` to a defined `backend.entities` for `firestorePaths` entries), a dedicated `admin_roles/{uid}` collection (as recommended by the "Global Roles (DBAC): Prefer Existence over Content" mandate) cannot be explicitly listed in the `firestorePaths` output. 

Consequently, security rules requiring an admin check (e.g., to allow `list` access to the `feedback` collection for administrators) will need to rely on the `role` field within the user's `UserProfile` document (`get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == 'admin'`). This constitutes a **necessary compromise** of the "Authorization Independence (CRITICAL): AVOID hierarchical authorization dependencies (`get()`)." principle. While functional, it introduces a `get()` call dependency into rules, which is less atomic and harder to debug compared to an `exists()` check on a dedicated `admin_roles` collection. The `UserProfile.role` field will serve this purpose for security rule evaluation, in addition to any application-level role logic.
